Waste Management System Rajshahi City Corporation
Overview

Secondary Transfer
Stations

Rajshahi City Corporation (RCC) has established multiple modern Secondary Transfer Stations (STSs) to manage daily municipal waste (~420 tons) hygienically. These stations act as intermediate storage points to efficiently transfer waste to the final landfill, aiming to reduce environmental pollution in residential areas.

  • Modernization InitiativeRCC plans to construct 30 STSs in phases to upgrade waste management from traditional dumping methods.
  • Key LocationsExisting modern STSs are in Terakhadia, Kazla, Sapura, Paba, Seroil Railway Station, Kashiadanga, Padma, and Shalbagan.
  • FunctionalityCrucial nodes where waste is collected, temporarily stored, and transferred — minimizing disposal in public areas.
  • Recent DevelopmentsNew stations have been opened to meet growing city demands, with ongoing operational improvements.
© Rajshahi City Corporation — Waste Management System
